notableMoments: Archie Gemmill scored one of the greatest World Cup goals of all time against the Netherlands in 1978, a mesmerizing solo run and chip that remains the defining image of Scottish World Cup history
Scotland have appeared at eight World Cups but have never advanced beyond the group stage, a painful record that fuels the nation's determination to break through in 2026
The 1978 campaign in Argentina was marked by Ally MacLeod's over-confident predictions and Scotland's dramatic failure to progress despite beating the Netherlands 3-2
Scotland's last World Cup appearance was in 1998 in France, making the 2026 tournament their first in 28 years and one of the most anticipated moments in Scottish sporting history
The famous 'Tartan Army' of traveling Scottish fans is renowned as one of the most passionate and good-natured supporter groups in world football
In 1974, Scotland were the only unbeaten team to be eliminated from the World Cup, drawing with Brazil and Yugoslavia before narrowly missing out on goal difference
Andy Robertson's captaincy and the emergence of Scott McTominay, Billy Gilmour, and John McGinn have given Scotland their strongest squad in a generation
Scotland qualified for the 2026 World Cup through European qualification, ending their longest-ever absence from the tournament and sparking wild celebrations across the country
